epistemic_basis: The anatomical description permits a finite integer answer frame with explicit unresolved mass.

answer_contract_outline.objective: Select the integer that matches the anatomical description.

response.reason: The requested output is a typed exact answer.

answer_contract.objective: Select the integer that matches the anatomical description.

hypotheses.statement: The anatomical structure has one relevant component.
falsifiers: The description requires more than one component.
predictions: One component accounts for all described attachments.

hypotheses.statement: The anatomical structure has two relevant components.
falsifiers: The description requires a count other than two.
predictions: Two components account for all described attachments.

hypotheses.statement: The anatomical structure has three relevant components.
falsifiers: The description requires a count other than three.
predictions: Three components account for all described attachments.

response.coverage_statement: The initial candidates are provisional and reserve unresolved mass for omitted counts.
response.coverage_limitation: The initial frame omits counts greater than three.

proposals.inquiry_goal: Check whether the stated attachments are compatible with any initially named count.
proposals.expected_observation: A count constraint that either fits one named candidate or exposes an omitted count.

reframe_condition.frame: All named counts are anatomically inconsistent.

response.raw_content: MODEL REASONING: the anatomical description requires more attachments than counts one, two, or three can provide, so every initial candidate is inconsistent.

response.unexplained_observation: No initial count can satisfy the attachment pattern.
response.interpretation: The first frame is missing a higher-count alternative.

candidates.statement: The anatomical structure has four relevant components.
falsifiers: A required attachment is absent from a four-component account.
predictions: Four components account for the described attachment pattern.
candidates.why_current_frame_missed: The initial frame stopped at three.
candidates.required_next_probe: Compare the four-component and five-component attachment maps.

candidates.statement: The anatomical structure has five relevant components.
falsifiers: A required attachment is absent from a five-component account.
predictions: Five components account for the described attachment pattern.
candidates.why_current_frame_missed: The initial frame stopped at three.
candidates.required_next_probe: Compare the four-component and five-component attachment maps.

proposals.inquiry_goal: Discriminate the expanded four-component and five-component candidates against the retained frame.
proposals.expected_observation: The attachment map fits four components and excludes five.

support_condition.H_exp_f2_1: Four components cover every described attachment.

weaken_condition.H_exp_f2_2: Five components introduce an unsupported attachment.

reframe_condition.frame: Neither expanded count matches the attachment map.

response.raw_content: MODEL REASONING: the expansion follow-up finds that four components complete the attachment map while five requires an absent attachment.
MODEL REASONING: the strongest falsifier check finds no missing attachment in the four-component account, while five requires an absent attachment.

response.interpretation: The expanded four-component candidate explains the complete map better than five or the original counts.
The strongest stated falsifier for the four-component account was not observed.
